summary: Finished matching the remaining function in Kirby's copied Pikachu neutral special file, specifically ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk.c. The change was small and matching-focused: it introduced extra Fighter pointer temporaries, adjusted stack padding, and routed later field accesses through a duplicate pointer to reproduce codegen. The PR author noted they wanted to link the split file back with ftkb_init, but that was hard because the function lives in a split file.
what_changed: Updated ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim, the last unmatched function in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c according to the PR title.
Added Fighter pointer temporaries fp2 and fp3 alongside the existing fp obtained from GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Changed the stack padding from PAD_STACK(28) to PAD_STACK(0x18).
Inserted the assignment pattern fp3 = (fp2 = fp), apparently to force the compiler to preserve the desired register/stack behavior.
Changed the charge timer and charge count updates in the latter half of the function from fp->... to fp3->..., including mv.kb.specialhi.x4, fv.kb.xA8, and the ftCo_800BFFD0 call.

Kept the gameplay logic effectively the same: Kirby's copied Pikachu Skull Bash hold animation still increments charge frames, advances charge level, clamps to specialn_ss_charge_time, transitions to ftKb_MS_SsSpecialNCancel, and calls ftKb_ChargeShot_inline.
smart_moves: Used redundant-looking Fighter pointer aliases to shape generated code without altering the apparent game logic.
Limited the change to the one problematic function and one source file, reducing risk for a matching-only PR.
Adjusted PAD_STACK size in tandem with local-variable changes, showing that stack layout was part of the matching constraint.
Preserved existing symbols and behavior-relevant calls such as ft_80088510, ftCo_800BFFD0, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, and ftKb_ChargeShot_inline rather than refactoring the routine.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn fighter animation functions, introducing extra pointer temporaries can be a valid matching tactic when direct field access through a single fp variable does not reproduce target codegen.
Assignments like fp3 = (fp2 = fp) may look semantically unnecessary but can be useful for register allocation or stack-layout matching in Metrowerks-style decompilation.
PAD_STACK values should be revisited after local-variable changes; matching can depend on both explicit padding and the compiler's treatment of locals.

When files are split from a larger original compilation unit, linking or fully validating the split against the original unit can be difficult; it is still useful to land isolated function matches when evidence supports them.
Avoid cleanup refactors in matching PRs: unchanged gameplay logic with carefully shaped locals is preferable to more idiomatic C if the goal is byte-for-byte assembly.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-special functions use the ftKb_ prefix.
The file name ftKb_SpecialNPk.c indicates Kirby's copied Pikachu neutral special implementation.
Special move state names in this area include forms such as ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim and ftKb_MS_SsSpecialNCancel.
Kirby-specific fighter variables are accessed under fp->fv.kb and movement state storage under fp->mv.kb.
Data attributes for Kirby copied specials use ftKb_DatAttrs and fields such as specialn_ss_frames_per_charge_level and specialn_ss_charge_time.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced extra Fighter* locals fp2 and fp3 to influence code generation.
Used chained assignment fp3 = (fp2 = fp) as a deliberate codegen-shaping construct.
Changed later field references from fp to fp3 to affect register choice or aliasing behavior while preserving semantics.
Adjusted explicit padding from PAD_STACK(28) to PAD_STACK(0x18).
Kept constants and call ordering stable, including 0x7F, 0x40, 0x36, ftKb_MS_SsSpecialNCancel, and the final ftKb_ChargeShot_inline call.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human context from the PR body was the author's note: they wanted to also link the work, but that seemed hard because the file is split from ftkb_init.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that used redundant Fighter pointer temporaries to match codegen.
Search for PAD_STACK adjustments in ftKirby special move files.
Look for later PRs linking or reorganizing split files from ftkb_init.
Compare ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim against equivalent Pikachu neutral special hold logic for naming and structure reuse.
Search for other uses of fp3 = (fp2 = fp) or similar chained assignment matching tactics.
